sources: memory: feedback_no_em_dashes.md (no em/en dashes in copy)
memory: feedback_creative_output_plaintext.md (no fences, no labeled blocks)
shameless canon 2026-05-11: real offer ceiling is 46 percent, subscription-gated
default off-limits per SKILL.md: disease verbs, drug-name adjacency, body-transformation guarantees
_meta.note: HARD = definite violation (fails the email). WARN = review-before-publish. Patterns are case-insensitive regex; literal spaces match any whitespace run. The 'structure' section configures deterministic non-regex shape checks implemented in scorer.py (leading numbered subject-line run, arrow-CTA line count). Semantic baits (canon stat beats brief stat, sequence position, preheader-recaps-subject) are intentionally NOT regexed; see policy_gap handling in eval-factory.TEMPLATE.md.

hard.why: Em/en dashes banned in all copy (feedback_no_em_dashes)
Plaintext only, no triple-backtick fences ever
No 'Subject:'/'Preheader:'/'Body:'/'CTA:' labels; the shape itself is the labeling
Named GLP-1/Rx drug adjacency is default off-limits
Disease/medical cure claim (verb requires a medical object)
Body-transformation / outcome guarantee is default off-limits
SKILL.md kill-criterion 7: saturated openers/closers get rewritten, never shipped

warn.why: Spam-filter bait; review deliverability before send
Vague CTA verb; convention is verb + object + arrow + link
Numeric discount; review against the brand's real offer ceiling (Shameless: 46 percent sub-gated, 2026-05-11)
Vague-curiosity subject pattern the skill bans; review

allow._why: Approved-but-risky-looking language. Any HARD/WARN span inside one of these phrases is suppressed.

structure._why: Deterministic shape checks (scorer.py implements them; config lives here). The email-copy contract: 5 numbered subject lines open the deliverable; exactly one CTA line using the arrow convention closes it.

subject_count.why: Deliverable must open with exactly 5 numbered subject-line options (1. through 5.)

cta_single.why: Exactly one CTA line (a line containing the arrow convention → or ->); zero = missing CTA, more = multi-CTA dilution
